summary: Fixed the QA-blocking locale canonicalization defect. The client i18n foundation now canonicalizes supported locale families instead of relying on a narrow alias list: en and en-* resolve to en-US, and zh and zh-* resolve to zh-CN unless a future Human Gate adds another Chinese locale. Web locale persistence now safely migrates persisted en-* and zh-* values to supported preferences, and localStorage getItem/setItem failures fall back without blocking boot. No sim-core, sim-ai, protocol, save-format, map-renderer, Electron IPC/preload, dependency, gate document, or product-scope path was changed.

tests_run.result: Vitest passed: 1 file, 13 tests. Added en/en-* and zh/zh-* family canonicalization coverage including en-GB before zh-Hans, zh-TW, zh-HK, and zh-Hans-HK.
Vitest passed: 1 file, 7 tests. Added Web persistence migration for zh-Hans, zh-TW, en-GB, system fallback for en-GB/zh-HK/zh-Hans-HK, and localStorage getItem/setItem throw protection.
Playwright passed: 14/14. Existing system zh-Hans fallback, manual switch, persistence, document.lang, viewport, keyboard, and core UI flows remain green.
Format check, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary check passed.

decisions.decision: Replaced the narrow Simplified Chinese alias list with supported language-family canonicalization. en and every en-* BCP-47 tag canonicalize to en-US; zh and every zh-* tag canonicalize to zh-CN.
Persisted legacy or regional locale values such as en-GB, zh-Hans, zh-TW, zh-HK, and zh-Hans-HK are parsed into the supported preference set before being rewritten to localStorage.
localStorage access remains a UI convenience. getItem failure falls back to system; setItem failure is swallowed so locale preference persistence cannot block Web shell boot.

The R2 repair changed only UI/Web/test code in allowed paths. It did not add dependencies, Electron IPC/preload surface, protocol fields, save fields, simulation behavior, map-renderer behavior, or product scope.

risks.summary: All zh-* system tags now map to zh-CN. This intentionally follows the current supported surface and product directive, but it is not a cultural/language-content decision that traditional Chinese or region-specific Chinese support is complete.
risks.mitigation: Future Traditional Chinese, regional Chinese, or additional locale support must go through a Human Gate/task update before changing SUPPORTED_CLIENT_LOCALE_FAMILIES.

risks.summary: This R2 repair only fixes locale negotiation/persistence. Full player-mode text cleanup remains assigned to downstream M7 UI quality tasks.
risks.mitigation: Keep M7 app shell, map, inspector, appointment, guidance, regression matrix, and UI-localization exit tasks responsible for final no-bare-key/no-debug-string coverage.

requested_action: Review the R2 locale-family canonicalization policy and confirm it satisfies the accepted systems boundary: client-only i18n, no sim/protocol/save/map authority changes, no Electron IPC/preload expansion, and current zh-* -> zh-CN behavior scoped to the present supported locale set. After systems review, route back to qa_reviewer for R2 verification.
